items.title: The Federal IDR Final Operations Rule is Out: Now What? - Modern Healthcare
items.reason: The item directly addresses the Federal IDR Final Operations Rule, which is a core CMS/HHS operational rule that governs how out-of-network payment disputes are arbitrated under the No Surprises Act—a primary topic in the relevance rubric.
items.summary: Modern Healthcare reports on the release of the Federal IDR Final Operations Rule, which establishes CMS/HHS operational procedures for the Independent Dispute Resolution process under the No Surprises Act. The rule governs how out-of-network payment disputes between providers and payers are arbitrated through certified Independent Dispute Resolution Entities (IDREs). This operational guidance directly impacts healthcare revenue-cycle teams by clarifying the mechanics of the IDR process, including portal requirements, arbitration procedures, and dispute eligibility determinations. The final rule represents a significant regulatory development that revenue-cycle teams must understand to navigate out-of-network payment disputes and ensure compliance with federal surprise billing protections.

also.title: Federal Agencies Finalize Overhaul of No Surprises Act Dispute Resolution Process - ArentFox Schiff
